validated_commands.quick_start: uv sync --frozen && uv run pytest -m "not slow" && uv run mypy
notes: uv installable via curl; needs PATH update after install
uv sync --frozen installs exact pinned deps from uv.lock
benchmark fixture comes from pytest-codspeed; DO NOT use -p no:codspeed (breaks benchmark tests)
slow marker deselects long-running tests; benchmark tests are NOT marked slow
uv run pytest -m 'not slow' = 187 passed (benchmark tests included, pass via codspeed fixture)

open_prs.title: Partial zeroing in BlockProcessor.reset() — zero only used array ranges
open_prs.note: Not in CodSpeed benchmarks. Real-world: 40-60x faster reset for short sessions. 187 tests pass, mypy/ruff clean.

closed_prs.outcome: MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-06. Batch BS1770 filter in write_all. CodSpeed confirmed: 88200Hz +23.6%, 96000Hz +17.55%, 44100/48000Hz +11.5%.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-04. square_and_sum() helper via np.einsum. CodSpeed: integrated +45%, momentary/short_term/lra +35-37%.
MERGED by nocarryr on 2026-05-03. Fused square-sum. ~2% improvement.

optimization_backlog.desc: BlockProcessor.reset() partial-zeroing: zero only [:block_index] for large arrays. PR #122 submitted 2026-05-07.
optimization_backlog.impact: 40-60x faster reset for short sessions. Not in CodSpeed (reset not benchmarked).

optimization_backlog.desc: Vendoring/trimming _upfirdn_apply.pyx. TruePeak FIR is ~34% of write_all time. Maintainer exploring in PR #116.
optimization_backlog.impact: Up to 34% of Meter.write (dominant bottleneck after PR#119 merged)

optimization_backlog.desc: lk_log10() scalar path via math.log10. ~1% only, below CodSpeed threshold.

maintainer_notes: PR #120 (revert-codspeed-parallel) open: maintainer investigating CodSpeed benchmark instability with parallel runs
PR #121 merged 2026-05-06: fixed RNG seed in random_samples fixture to make benchmarks deterministic
2026-05-04: 'Read and address review comments in #117' - DONE
2026-05-05: 'Add explanatory comment above block_filtered/block_unfiltered in write_all' - DONE
perf_notes: CodSpeed threshold ~10% CPU instruction improvement to register as meaningful.
BlockProcessor.reset() full 144K-element zeroing: ~136 µs. Partial (10-block session): ~3 µs (41x speedup). PR #122.
sosfilt batching in write_all: 46 calls -> 1 = 1.85x faster filter step. CodSpeed: +11-23% (PR #119 merged).
Post-PR#119 profile (48kHz, 1s, 10 runs): TruePeak FIR 34%, sosfilt 14%, reset 10%, _write 6%, processing 6%.
TruePeakProcessor uses scipy._upfirdn_apply._apply (Cython). No Python-level speedup possible.
np.einsum('ij,ij->i', a, a) = sum-of-squares without temp array. PR #117: +45%/+35-37% CodSpeed.
RunningSum->float: NO improvement (tested 2026-05-02). Do not retry.
CodSpeed simulation mode counts CPU instructions - Python dispatch overhead matters.
DO NOT use -p no:codspeed when running tests - breaks benchmark fixture.
random_samples fixture now uses fixed seed (PR #121 merged 2026-05-06) - benchmarks more deterministic.

infrastructure_notes.codspeed_threshold: ~10% CPU instruction improvement to register as meaningful
infrastructure_notes.benchmark_coverage: 18 benchmarks: block_processor (4 modes), meter (4 rates), TruePeakProcessor (48kHz 4x, 88.2kHz 2x), filter benchmarks
infrastructure_notes.codspeed_instability: Maintainer investigating in PR #120 (parallel runs cause instability); PR #121 fixed RNG seed
